LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF KEESUNG NAM'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. THERE IS NO UNDUE DELAY. THERE IS NO EVIDENCE OF PREJUDICE. WITH TRIAL SET FOR MAY OF 2021, THERE IS SUFFICIENT TIME FOR DISCOVERY AND DISPOSITIVE MOTIONS. THE NEW COMPLAINT SHOULD BE FILED AND SERVED NOT LATER THAN AUGUST, 31 2020. ORDER SIGNED. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-30-2019 Overruled
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Ken Stratton, Esq. of RLG 415-432-7990 for Plaintiff.
Cara Mae Acibo, Esq. of Hoge Fenton 408-287-9501 for Defendant STEPHEN MOSKOWITZ, MOSKOWITZ, LLP, MOSKOWITZ LAW CORPORATION.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares its views. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT STEPHEN MOSKOWITZ, MOSKOWITZ, LLP, MOSKOWITZ LAW CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendants' demurrer to Plaintiff's complaint as to the second cause of action for violation of Civil Code 1632 is overruled. The California Translation Act provides that any person engaged in a trade or business who negotiates primarily in certain specified foreign languages including Korean, "orally or in writing in the course of entering into any of the following, shall deliver to the other party to the contract or agreement and prior to the execution thereof a translation of the contract or agreement in the language in which the contract or agreement was negotiatied, that includes a translation of every term and condition in that contract or agreement." (Civ. Code 1632(b).) The Act specifically applies to attorneys' fees agreements. ( 1632(b)(6).) Plaintiff alleges he speaks Korean, that Defendants knew this and therefore provided a Korean translator during their negotiations. (2d. Amend. Compl. 12-13, 19-21, 39-40.) Plaintiff alleges the terms were orally negotiated in Korean and formalized in signed written agreements. (Id. at 39-41.) These written agreements were in English only; Defendants never provided Plaintiff with a Korean translation, thereby violating the California Translation Act. (Id.) If an attorney fails to provide the translation required by statute, the client may rescind the contract. (Civ. Code, 1632(k) ["Upon a failure to comply with the provisions of this section, the person aggrieved may rescind the contract or agreement in the manner provided by this chapter."]; California Practice Guide: Professional Responsibility (The Rutter Guide 2019) 5:619 [same].) Plaintiff further alleges Defendants took no action on Plaintiff's behalf regarding the second fee agreement from 2016, and that three days after confirming the receipt of the $200,000 flat fee, Defendants informed Plaintiff that he did not qualify for the treaty "tiebreaker" position. (2d. Amend. Compl. 27-28.) Plaintiff also alleges Defendants did not complete certain unfinished services under the first fee agreement. (Id. at 30.)(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
